Class PathConfig

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.settings.PathConfig
public final class PathConfig extends Object

Keeps some central paths to files and directories.

By default all KeY configurations are stored in a directory named ".key" inside the user's home directory. In Microsoft windows operating systems this is directly the hard disc that contains the KeY code. But the eclipse integration requires to change the default location. This is possible via setKeyConfigDir(String) which should be called once before something is done with KeY (e.g. before the MainWindow is opened).

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    DISREGARD_SETTINGS_PROPERTY
    The name of the Java system property used to indicate that the settings in the KeY directory should not be consulted at startup.
    static final String
    KEY_DIRECTORY_NAME
    The default name of the directory that contains KeY settings.
